english_script: Hi guys, it's Lulu. Day 31 of my daily English practice. I keep talking to improve my oral English. Today I watched a YouTube video from Mind the Gap. It was about learning vocabulary the right way. The video explained that vocabulary is like fuel for your English car. Without fuel, you can't go anywhere. But fuel alone is not enough. You also need a gearbox, which is collocations. And you need road rules, which is grammar. All three work together. The speakers said your vocabulary size decides how much English you can understand. This is called comprehensible input. If you know more words, you can understand news, podcasts, and films better. It also gives you precision in speaking. If you only have a few ways to say something, your speech is limited. From this I learned that learning words alone is not enough. You need to learn how words fit together. For example, we say 'strong coffee', not 'powerful coffee'. That is a collocation. The video shared three tips for learning vocabulary. First, learn words in chunks, not alone. Second, use the words in real sentences. Third, review them often. This helps move words from short-term to long-term memory. The speakers also said many learners freeze when speaking. They know the word but can't say it. This happens because the word is not active. To make words active, you need to practice speaking them. The video suggested shadowing. That means repeating what you hear right away. It helps with pronunciation and fluency. I also learned that you don't need a huge vocabulary to speak well. You need the right vocabulary. Focus on common words and how they are used. The speakers gave an example. They said 'take a break' is better than 'have a rest' in some situations. Small details like this make your English sound natural. The video was very helpful for me. I will try to learn words in chunks from now on. I will also practice shadowing more. This is a good way to improve my speaking. Thank you for listening, see you tomorrow.
chinese_translation: 大家好，我是Lulu。每日英语练习的第31天。我坚持说话来提高我的英语口语。今天我看了Mind the Gap的一个YouTube视频。它讲的是如何正确学习词汇。视频解释说，词汇就像你英语车的燃料。没有燃料，你哪儿也去不了。但光有燃料不够。你还需要一个变速箱，那就是搭配。你还需要道路规则，那就是语法。三者一起工作。主持人说你的词汇量决定你能理解多少英语。这叫做可理解输入。如果你知道更多单词，你就能更好地理解新闻、播客和电影。它还能让你说话更精确。如果你只有一两种方式表达，你的语言就很有限。从中我学到，只学单词是不够的。你需要学习单词如何搭配。例如，我们说'strong coffee'，而不是'powerful coffee'。这就是搭配。视频分享了三个学习词汇的技巧。第一，成组学习单词，不要单独学。第二，在真实句子中使用它们。第三，经常复习。这有助于将单词从短期记忆转移到长期记忆。主持人还说很多人在说话时会卡住。他们知道这个词但说不出来。这是因为这个词没有被激活。要让词汇活跃起来，你需要练习说出来。视频建议跟读。这意味着立刻重复你听到的内容。它有助于发音和流利度。我还学到，你不需要很大的词汇量就能说得好。你需要正确的词汇。专注于常用词及其用法。主持人举了个例子。他们说在某些情况下'take a break'比'have a rest'更好。像这样的小细节能让你的英语听起来更自然。这个视频对我很有帮助。从现在开始我会尝试成组学习单词。我也会更多地练习跟读。这是提高口语的好方法。谢谢聆听，明天见。
concise_english: Hi guys, it's Lulu. Day 31 of my daily English practice. I keep talking to improve my oral English. Today I watched a video from Mind the Gap. It was about learning vocabulary. The video said vocabulary is like fuel for a car. You also need collocations and grammar. All three work together. The speakers shared three tips. First, learn words in chunks. Second, use them in real sentences. Third, review often. This helps you remember words. You don't need a huge vocabulary. You need the right words. Practice speaking them to make them active. I will try these tips. Thank you for listening, see you tomorrow.
concise_chinese: 大家好，我是Lulu。每日英语练习的第31天。我坚持说话来提高我的英语口语。今天我看了Mind the Gap的一个视频。它讲的是学习词汇。视频说词汇就像汽车的燃料。你还需要搭配和语法。三者一起工作。主持人分享了三个技巧。第一，成组学习单词。第二，在真实句子中使用它们。第三，经常复习。这有助于你记住单词。你不需要很大的词汇量。你需要正确的单词。练习说出来让它们变得活跃。我会尝试这些技巧。谢谢聆听，明天见。
